old_claim.note: Basic Chemistry (Early Forms) is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained because the source-backed Tyrian-purple chronology predates the broad Classical basic-chemistry node.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and results identify purple-dyed wool fibers from Timna Valley dated to around 1000 BCE; the graph basic-chemistry node remains anchored later in the Classical era.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Iron Age royal-purple dyeing is direct craft evidence and should not be made dependent on a later broad chemistry node.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Dye extraction and mordant/dyeing craft knowledge are real prerequisites, but the existing broad Basic Chemistry node is too late and too abstract for a direct dependency.

ontology_before: Tyrian-purple dyeing treated Basic Chemistry (Early Forms) as a historical predecessor.
ontology_after: Tyrian-purple dyeing remains connected to textiles, sailing, and animal/resource systems without a false later chemistry dependency.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: Tyrian-purple dyeing must not directly depend on Basic Chemistry (Early Forms) unless that chemistry node is re-scoped and redated to earlier dye-craft chemistry.
would_reject_if: The Basic Chemistry node is re-scoped and source-anchored to pre-1000 BCE dye chemistry.
The Tyrian-purple node is narrowed to later theoretical chemistry of dyes rather than craft dye extraction.
A stronger source shows the Timna royal-purple evidence should not anchor the node chronology.
short_reason: Source-backed Tyrian-purple dyeing predates the broad Classical basic-chemistry node.
